Some of the smartest people on the planet have marshalled their talents (and trillions of dollars) to move the world beyond fossil fuels. So why have they accomplished … almost nothing? Between 2015 and 2023, the world invested over $12 trillion in alternative energy. By the end of that period, we were investing nearly twice as much in renewables as we were in fossil fuels. The result: Between 2013 and 2023 the percentage of global energy derived from fossil fuels declined from 82% … to 80%. What explains fossil fuels’ continued dominance as the world’s primary energy source? For starters, fossil fuels are efficient. A single oil well in the Permian Basin of West Texas generates as much energy as *ten* 330-feet high windmills. Second, they’re reliable. Coal and natural gas electric plants can generate their maximum amount of power far more often than either wind or solar plants. Third, storage. Storing energy from fossil fuels for a month is about 30 times less expensive than using the batteries required for renewable power. Also, America’s largest use of energy is for transportation, nearly 90% of which comes from oil-based fuels. Why? Because alternatives can’t match their perfor …

This past month, my family celebrated 50 years in the offshore marine service industry, operating crew boats in support of oil & gas exploration in the Gulf of America. Together, we've weathered the industry's highs and lows—booms, crashes, hurricanes, and regulations. Through resilience, we've learned that tough times never last, but tough people do. That resilience isn't just my family's story—it's the story of Louisiana itself, where our people face challenges head-on with unwavering determination and grit. My father always taught me to seize opportunity while saving for the inevitable rainy day - wisdom that has served our industry well. Through five decades and multiple industry downturns, we've honored every commitment and obligation. That's the Louisiana way. I'm forever grateful for this industry that has supported my family and countless others across South Louisiana for generations. Our communities, churches, and schools have all been built by the hard work of oil and gas families just like mine. Energy powers every aspect of American life, from keeping the lights on to fueling our economy. Yet, restrictive federal and state policies have driven up costs, limited production, and made energy less reliable for families and businesses. With a new administration in office, major policy shifts are underway to reverse these harmful regulations and restore energy independence. But will these changes be enough to stabilize the industry, lower prices, and ensure long-term energy security? In this episode of American Potential, host David From sits down with energy policy expert Faith Burns to break down the federal actions reshaping America’s energy landscape. They discuss how the administration is rolling back costly regulations, the role of Congress in securing stable policies, and the state-level mandates that continue to inflate energy prices. With energy costs directly impacting everything from groceries to transportation, this conversation highlights why an all-of-the-above energy strategy is crucial for economic growth and affordability. Tune in to learn how these changes could shape America’s energy future—and what still needs to be done.

As a result, they were forced to pay $12,974,250 in Alternative Compliance Payments to the State of Delaware. 💸 This nearly $13 million went into the state’s Green Energy Fund, used for projects like home weatherization, LED light bulbs, and small solar panel grants.
